name: breaking-news@dscc.org
subject: What Mitch McConnell didn't want Elizabeth Warren to say
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Mitch McConnell silenced Elizabeth Warren from opposing Jeff Sessions on the Senate floor. Add your name to show Democrats have her back..
---
[](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954ecd3/2b07114c/44e464e7/73695efa/1836104811/VEsH/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stand-with-elizabeth-warren?source=PT-20170208-Breaking-Pros")
Friend --

Tonight, the Senate voted 49-43 to silence Elizabeth Warren for making a speech about Jeff Sessions -- Trump's pick for Attorney General.

Here's what happened:

Thirty one years ago, Ronald Reagan nominated Sessions to be a federal judge. Coretta Scott King, the widow of Dr. Martin Luther King, wrote a letter opposing that nomination, saying that Sessions, "used the power of his office as United States Attorney to intimidate and chill the free exercise of the ballot by citizens."

And tonight, Sen. Warren tried to read that letter on the floor of the Senate.

In the middle of her speech, GOP Majority Leader Mitch McConnell cut her off, suggested that she had "impugned the motives and conduct of our colleague from Alabama," and invoked a Senate rule to silence her.

**[Right now, we need to personally ask you to stand with Senator Warren, friend.](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954ecd3/2b07114c/44e464e7/73695efa/1836104811/VEsE/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stand-with-elizabeth-warren?source=PT-20170208-Breaking-Pros")**

Below is the full text of the letter from King so you can see exactly what Mitch McConnell thought the Senate shouldn't hear.

> _"I write to express my sincere opposition to the confirmation of Jefferson B. Sessions as a federal district court judge for the Southern District of Alabama. My professional and personal roots in Alabama are deep and lasting. Anyone who has used the power of his office as United States Attorney to intimidate and chill the free exercise of the ballot by citizens should not be elevated to our courts. Mr. Sessions has used the awesome powers of his office in a shabby attempt to intimidate and frighten elderly black voters. For this reprehensible conduct, he should not be rewarded with a federal judgeship.
>
> I regret that a long-standing commitment prevents me from appearing in person to testify against this nominee. However, I have attached a copy of my statement opposing Mr. Sessions' confirmation and I request that my statement as well as this be made a part of the hearing record.
>
> I do sincerely urge you to oppose the confirmation of Mr. Sessions. "_

The final vote on confirmation for Sessions is expected to come some time Wednesday.

Our Democratic Senators are right now holding the floor for 30 hours of debate as a way to register opposition -- that's why Sen. Warren was making this speech.

Will you add your name, friend?

**[https://dscc.org/stand-with-elizabeth-warren](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954ecd3/2b07114c/44e464e7/73695efa/1836104811/VEsF/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stand-with-elizabeth-warren?source=PT-20170208-Breaking-Pros")**

Thank you,

Team DSCC

name: Donna Brazile
subject: re: Coretta Scott King's letter
body:
Republicans silenced Elizabeth Warren for trying to read this
---
**Last night, Senate Republicans voted to silence Elizabeth Warren for attempting to read Coretta Scott King's letter opposing Jeff Sessions. Scroll down to read her letter for yourself, and then call on the Senate to reject Jeff Sessions.

** **[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Reject-Jeff-Sessions1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170208_db_s2&source=em_20170208_db_s2")**

Friend --

Last night, while Senate Democrats held the floor to protest Trump's nomination of Jeff Sessions to be Attorney General, Sen. Elizabeth Warren attempted to read a letter that Coretta Scott King wrote in 1986 to oppose his nomination to be a federal judge.

Then, 49 Republicans voted to silence her.

King wrote to the Senate because Sessions "used the power of his office as United States Attorney to intimidate and chill the free exercise of the ballot by citizens." Now, Trump wants to make Sessions the chief law enforcement official in our country, responsible for safeguarding our right to vote.

Americans deserve to know the truth about Jeff Sessions. Scroll down to read the letter Mitch McConnell and the Senate GOP didn't want you to hear last night -- **then add your name before today's vote and demand the Senate reject Sessions' nomination.**

**[ADD YOUR NAME](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Reject-Jeff-Sessions1?keycode=&firstname=\(null\)&lastname=\(null\)&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20902&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20170208_db_s2&source=em_20170208_db_s2")**
Thanks,

Donna

Donna Brazile
Chair
Democratic National Committee

* * *

_Dear Senator Thurmond:

I write to express my sincere opposition to the confirmation of Jefferson B. Sessions as a federal district court judge for the Southern District of Alabama. My professional and personal roots in Alabama are deep and lasting. Anyone who has used the power of his office as United States Attorney to intimidate and chill the free exercise of the ballot by citizens should not be elevated to our courts. Mr. Sessions has used the awesome powers of his office in a shabby attempt to intimidate and frighten elderly black voters. For this reprehensible conduct, he should not be rewarded with a federal judgeship.

I regret that a long-standing commitment prevents me from appearing in person to testify against this nominee. However, I have attached a copy of my statement opposing Mr. Sessions' confirmation and I request that my statement as well as this be made a part of the hearing record.

I do sincerely urge you to oppose the confirmation of Mr. Sessions.

Sincerely,

Coretta Scott King

(**[Read her full testimony here.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c64e/4067b0a/2c103d647/7e7a327c/1844243375/VEsF/ "http://my.democrats.org/King-Testimony?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+Read+her+full+testimony+here&utm_campaign=em_20170208_db_s2&source=em_20170208_db_s2")**)

name: The White House
subject: Major Economic Update:
body:
Support Getting Americans Back to Work. |
[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=aa4923148d1747af207da6421ba681b0b6433ddcef8195cfe7107086eb2ddd0941d8ca22db83304521a3a3f39d5d9df286fe0c0b359d6ac2)

The past 8 years have taken a toll on America’s job force. But now, we have already begun bringing OUR jobs back to OUR country.

Today we are excited to announce that Intel has pledged to make a $7 billion investment in Arizona, creating 10,000 jobs!

Companies across America are making the commitment to creating thousands of new jobs everyday:
* 11/29/2016: Carrier pledges to create 1,000 new jobs
* 12/28/2016: Sprint and OneWeb announces it's creating and saving 8,000 jobs
* 01/17/2017: GM pledges $1 billion in manufacturing to create 1,500 new jobs

[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=aa4923148d1747af207da6421ba681b0b6433ddcef8195cfe7107086eb2ddd0941d8ca22db83304521a3a3f39d5d9df286fe0c0b359d6ac2)
President Trump made a promise to this country – bring jobs back to America. The spirit of optimism sweeping the country is already boosting job growth, and it is only the beginning.

Over the next 4 years, President Trump is committed to creating new policies and embracing challenges in the labor force that will put America First in job creation, growth and innovation.

We are getting America back to work. And with each new success, we continue to make America great again.

**[Support Getting Americans Back to Work](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=aa4923148d1747af207da6421ba681b0b6433ddcef8195cfe7107086eb2ddd0941d8ca22db83304521a3a3f39d5d9df286fe0c0b359d6ac2)**

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Elizabeth Warren & Coretta Scott King
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello -- Last night, Senator Warren spoke on the Senate floor to oppose Senator Jeff Sessions' nomination for Attorney General by reading a letter that civil rights leader, Coretta Scott King, wrote in 1986.
Senator Warren spoke out until Republican Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell silenced her, suggesting that Warren's use of Coretta Scott King's words were an attack on Senator Sessions.
Senator Sessions has a long and troubling history of standing against the human and civil rights of individuals, rights that are essential to our civic and economic progress as a country. To silence Elizabeth Warren is not only insulting to the memory of Coretta Scott King, it is damaging to our democratic process.
[**If you believe that it is wrong for Senate Republicans like Mitch McConnell to silence Senator Warren from reading Coretta Scott King's words, add your name to tell them to let her speak.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd04/2a380d31/140eac8b/fed242/389492031/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20170208_stand-up-to-mcconnell-nd?source=e20170208_Warren_nd&firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&")
Thank you for getting involved.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan

Senator Warren spoke out until Republican Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell silenced her, suggesting that Warren's use of Coretta Scott King's words were an attack on Senator Sessions. Senator Sessions has a long and troubling history of standing against the human and civil rights of individuals, rights that are essential to our civic and economic progress as a country. To silence Elizabeth Warren is not only insulting to the memory of Coretta Scott King, it is damaging to our democratic process. "&" Thank you for getting involved. With every good wish, Maggie Hassan ""

name: OurRevolution.com
subject: Rally for Keith in Baltimore
body:
Rally to support Keith Ellison and a progressive agenda.
#
Fellow Maryland progressives,
Our Revolution Maryland is organizing and taking action in Maryland! We're proud to support Keith Ellison as the next Chair of the Democratic National Committee.
The DNC is holding a regional forum in Baltimore, and all the DNC Chair candidates will be there. [**Our Revolution members will rally to support Keith Ellison and a progressive agenda for the Democratic Party. Can you join us on Saturday, February 11?**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0bca/43f17890/23c1c9b8/6200581e/2221872828/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/volunteeractivityormeeting/gplwpd")
**Rally @ DNC Regional Forum in Baltimore**
Saturday, February 11
11:30am
Baltimore Convention Center
1 W. Pratt Street
Baltimore, MD 21201
Immediately following the rally, the DNC is holding one of its forums for all the candidates. The time of the forum is from 1 p.m. to 6 p.m., with the expectation that the chair candidates will go first and that part will end by 3:30 p.m. We would like to get as many supporters there as possible.
If we want to transform the Democratic Party to make it once again responsive to the needs of working people, we need Rep. Keith Ellison to serve as the next DNC Chair. [**Show your support for Keith and a people-powered Democratic Party -- RSVP to join us at the rally and the DNC Regional Forum.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0bca/43f17890/23c1c9b8/6200581f/2221872828/VEsE/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/regional-forums-2017")
**[RSVP to attend the rally and dnc forum»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0bca/43f17890/23c1c9b8/6200581e/2221872828/VEsF/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/volunteeractivityormeeting/gplwpd")**
We look forward to seeing you!
Our Revolution - Maryland Rising Steering Committee
_P.S.: You can also help support Keith by[signing and sharing our online petition](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0bca/43f17890/23c1c9b8/6200581c/2221872828/VEsC/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/keith-ellison-dnc?source=em170208-md")!_

PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
603 2ND ST, NE
WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a 501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution's general mission and are not designated for any specific activity.
603 2nd St NE, Washington, DC 20002

body:
Mitch McConnell silenced Elizabeth Warren from opposing Jeff Sessions on the Senate floor. Add your name to show Democrats have her back..
---
[****](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954ecdf/2b07114c/44f41779/73695a0b/207462557/VEsH/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stand-with-elizabeth-warren?source=PT-20170208-DidYouSeeDS-Pros")

Friend -- We wanted to make sure you saw this message from last night. Mitch McConnell silenced Elizabeth Warren on the Senate floor -- just for attempting to read a letter from Coretta Scott King regarding Attorney General nominee Jeff Sessions' civil rights record in Alabama.

His reasoning? _" She was warned. She was given an explanation. Nevertheless, she persisted."_ She sure did.

And so are we!

[**Will you sign on right now to show Senator Warren and Senate Democrats you have their back? Together, we 'll keep fighting, persisting, and speaking out for what's right (no matter how hard Mitch McConnell tries to shut us up).**](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954ecdf/2b07114c/44f41779/73695a0b/207462557/VEsE/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stand-with-elizabeth-warren?source=PT-20170208-DidYouSeeDS-Pros")

Thank you,
Team DSCC

\------------------
Forwarded message:
From: breaking-news@dscc.org
Date: Wednesday, February 8, 2017
Subject: What Mitch McConnell didn't want Elizabeth Warren to say

Friend --

Tonight, the Senate voted 49-43 to silence Elizabeth Warren for making a speech about Jeff Sessions -- Trump's pick for Attorney General.

Here's what happened:

Thirty one years ago, Ronald Reagan nominated Sessions to be a federal judge. Coretta Scott King, the widow of Dr. Martin Luther King, wrote a letter opposing that nomination, saying that Sessions, "used the power of his office as United States Attorney to intimidate and chill the free exercise of the ballot by citizens."

And tonight, Sen. Warren tried to read that letter on the floor of the Senate.

In the middle of her speech, GOP Majority Leader Mitch McConnell cut her off, suggested that she had "impugned the motives and conduct of our colleague from Alabama," and invoked a Senate rule to silence her.

**[Right now, we need to personally ask you to stand with Senator Warren, friend.](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954ecdf/2b07114c/44f41779/73695a0b/207462557/VEsF/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stand-with-elizabeth-warren?source=PT-20170208-DidYouSeeDS-Pros")**

Below is the full text of the letter from King so you can see exactly what Mitch McConnell thought the Senate shouldn't hear.

> _"I write to express my sincere opposition to the confirmation of Jefferson B. Sessions as a federal district court judge for the Southern District of Alabama. My professional and personal roots in Alabama are deep and lasting. Anyone who has used the power of his office as United States Attorney to intimidate and chill the free exercise of the ballot by citizens should not be elevated to our courts. Mr. Sessions has used the awesome powers of his office in a shabby attempt to intimidate and frighten elderly black voters. For this reprehensible conduct, he should not be rewarded with a federal judgeship.
>
> I regret that a long-standing commitment prevents me from appearing in person to testify against this nominee. However, I have attached a copy of my statement opposing Mr. Sessions' confirmation and I request that my statement as well as this be made a part of the hearing record.
>
> I do sincerely urge you to oppose the confirmation of Mr. Sessions. "_

The final vote on confirmation for Sessions is expected to come some time Wednesday.

Our Democratic Senators are right now holding the floor for 30 hours of debate as a way to register opposition -- that's why Sen. Warren was making this speech.

Will you add your name, friend?

**[https://dscc.org/stand-with-elizabeth-warren](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954ecdf/2b07114c/44f41779/73695a0b/207462557/VEsC/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/stand-with-elizabeth-warren?source=PT-20170208-DidYouSeeDS-Pros")**

Thank you,

Team DSCC

P.S. -- Forward this message to five of your friends so that everyone can see what Mitch McConnell thought the Senate shouldn't hear.

**Senate Republicans just voted to confirm Jeff Sessions as our next Attorney General. Pitch in today to stand with Democrats who will keep fighting and hold the Trump administration and the rest of the GOP accountable.

** **[PITCH IN](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c65a/4067b0a/2c0682963/7e7a258a/3856773554/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VFgwTlZVMVJQVFY5R1NVVk1SRnR6YkhWblBXdGxlV052WkdWZEpTVT0iOiIifQ==/ "http://my.democrats.org/Help-Democrats-Fight-Back1?keycode=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+PITCH+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170208_db2_nd&source=em_20170208_db2_nd")**

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: nevertheless, she persisted
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
Last night Republicans voted to silence Elizabeth Warren during her remarks on the Senate floor in opposition to President Trump's controversial Attorney General nominee. | __
Earlier today I rose to speak on the Senate floor in strong opposition to Jeff Sessions' nomination to be Attorney General. When my colleague Elizabeth Warren did the same last night, Republicans voted to silence her.

And it gets worse. She was silenced for reading a letter from Coretta Scott King, widow of Dr. Martin Luther King, opposing Sessions as a federal judge thirty-one years ago. King wrote that Sessions had "used the power of his office as United States Attorney to intimidate and chill the free exercise of the ballot by citizens."

[**Join me now to stand with Senator Warren and Senate Democrats to tell Republicans that Jeff Sessions' record matters -- and we will not let it be ignored.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b30/70cdc2ba/4741b0f5/397edfe/1753769908/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/sign-on-stand-with-elizabeth-warren-and-senate-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Join+me+now+to+stand+with+Senator+Warren&utm_campaign=em020817&source=em020817")

We need an Attorney General that will protect and defend the rights of the American people. Senator Sessions' record and statements raise serious concerns about his ability to carry out this sacred duty on behalf of every American and stand up to a president that has already demonstrated his disdain and disrespect for our courts and judges.

Senate Democrats will continue to raise our voices because we need a Justice Department that is able to check the anti-democratic tendencies of this president. Jeff Sessions is not the right choice.

[**Join us, please. Add your name.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b30/70cdc2ba/4741b0f5/397edff/1753769908/VEsE/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/sign-on-stand-with-elizabeth-warren-and-senate-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Join+us+FIRSTNAMEplease+Add+your+name&utm_campaign=em020817&source=em020817")

The final vote on confirmation is set to happen later today.

Keep fighting,

Chris

* * *

Full text of the letter from King that Mitch McConnell has tried to silence:

> __"I write to express my sincere opposition to the confirmation of Jefferson B. Sessions as a federal district court judge for the Southern District of Alabama. My professional and personal roots in Alabama are deep and lasting. Anyone who has used the power of his office as United States Attorney to intimidate and chill the free exercise of the ballot by citizens should not be elevated to our courts.
